description: Build lessons 2-9 for the AI learning platform. Each lesson teaches AI concepts to non-technical users with interactive sections, quizzes, and celebrations.

userStories.title: Update Dashboard with new lesson titles
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the correct lesson titles on the dashboard so I know what each lesson covers.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows updated titles: Lesson 2='How Software Works', Lesson 3='The AI Tools Landscape', Lesson 4='AI in Action', Lesson 5='Your First Build', Lesson 6='Building for Operations', Lesson 7='Data & AI', Lesson 8='???' (hidden/locked), Lesson 9='Final Project'
Lesson 8 shows a lock icon and mysterious '???' title
Lesson 8 is not clickable until unlocked
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Update src/pages/Dashboard.jsx or wherever lesson cards are rendered

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 page structure and routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the basic Lesson 2 page following Lesson 1's pattern so sections can be added.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/Lesson2.jsx following Lesson1.jsx pattern
Add route for /lesson/2 in the router
Lesson 2 has 8 sections defined: Introduction, Restaurant Analogy, Real Examples, Why This Matters for AI, Technical Describing Practice, Label the Parts, Quiz Time, Completion
Sidebar navigation works with section list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow exact pattern from Lesson1.jsx - sections array, sectionComponents mapping, navigation

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 Section 1 - Introduction
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the introduction for Lesson 2 so I understand what I'll learn.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sections/lesson2/Introduction.jsx
Shows welcome animation with software/code themed visuals
Displays heading: 'How Software Works'
Shows 3 learning objective cards with icons
Has motivational text about communicating with developers and AI
Has 'Let's begin' button that advances to next section
Uses Framer Motion for animations
Follows design system (purple/blue gradients, glassmorphism)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 Section 2 - Restaurant Analogy
userStories.description: As a user, I want to learn the restaurant analogy for software components.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sections/lesson2/RestaurantAnalogy.jsx
Shows heading 'Software is Like a Restaurant'
Has 4 interactive cards that reveal on click: Frontend (Dining Room), Backend (Kitchen), Database (Pantry), API (Waiters)
Each card shows visual, title, description, examples, and key trait
Cards animate on reveal using Framer Motion
Has animated flow showing order going through all 4 components
Shows Key Insight Box at bottom
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 Section 3 - Real Examples
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see real app examples (Vloto, Instagram, Google) broken into components.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sections/lesson2/RealExamples.jsx
Has tab component with 3 tabs: Vloto App, Instagram, Google Search
Each tab shows visual diagram with Frontend, Backend, Database, API labeled
Vloto tab has interactive mockup - click parts to see which component handles what
Shows comparison table at bottom comparing all 3 apps
Shows Key Insight Box
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 Section 4 - Why This Matters for AI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to understand why learning software concepts helps me use AI tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sections/lesson2/WhyThisMatters.jsx
Shows heading 'So Why Learn This?' with subheading
Has animated diagram showing old way (You->Developer->Months) vs new way (You->AI->Hours)
Shows 'The Catch' card explaining AI needs clear descriptions
Shows before/after example of bad vs good AI requests
Has 3 benefit cards: Talk to Developers, Direct AI Tools, Evaluate Solutions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 Section 5 - Technical Describing Practice
userStories.description: As a user, I want to practice describing tools using the 4-part framework.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sections/lesson2/TechnicalDescribing.jsx
Shows framework visual: What users SEE (Frontend), What system DOES (Backend), What's REMEMBERED (Database), What CONNECTS (API)
Has 3 interactive exercises with text areas or multiple choice for each component
Exercise 1: Describe a Todo App
Exercise 2: Describe a Booking Calendar
Exercise 3: Describe a Customer Feedback Tool
Each exercise has 'Check my answer' button showing comparison with good answer
Shows Pro Tip Box at bottom
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 Section 6 - Label the Parts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to test my understanding by labeling software components in screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sections/lesson2/LabelTheParts.jsx
Shows heading 'Test Your Eyes'
Has 3 interactive labeling exercises with mock screenshots
Screenshot 1: Login form - label username field (Frontend), check password (Backend), user accounts (Database), login request (API)
Screenshot 2: Newsfeed - label post cards (Frontend), sort by relevance (Backend), all posts (Database), fetch feed (API)
Screenshot 3: Booking confirmation - label confirmation page (Frontend), create booking (Backend), booking records (Database), send confirmation (API)
Shows feedback after each: 'Correct!' or 'Not quite - [explanation]'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 quiz data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the quiz questions for Lesson 2.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/data/quiz2Questions.js with 6 questions
Q1: Restaurant analogy - kitchen represents what? (Backend)
Q2: Tapping Vloto button interacts with what? (Frontend)
Q3: Booking history stored where? (Database)
Q4: What does API do? (Carries messages)
Q5: Adding car battery feature needs which parts? (Frontend, Backend, Database)
Q6: Why understand for AI tools? (Describe clearly)
Each question has options, correct answer, and feedback
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow pattern from quiz1Questions.js if it exists

userStories.title: Create Lesson 2 Section 7 - Quiz Time
userStories.description: As a user, I want to take a quiz to test my Lesson 2 knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/sections/lesson2/QuizTime.jsx
Uses quiz data from src/data/quiz2Questions.js
Shows one question at a time with 4 options
Highlights correct/incorrect after selection
Shows feedback for each answer
Tracks score and displays at end
Has 'Next Question' button progression
Saves quiz completion to Supabase progress
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Can reuse or extend QuizTime component pattern from Lesson 1

Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Final integration testing and polish
userStories.description: As a user, I want all lessons to work smoothly together.
acceptanceCriteria: All lesson routes work correctly
Progress saves and loads correctly for lessons 2-9
Navigation between lessons works
Sidebar progress tracking shows correct state
All quizzes save results to Supabase
Design system is consistent across all lessons
Animations are smooth with Framer Motion
Dev server runs without errors: npm run dev
Typecheck passes: npm run typecheck or tsc
Build succeeds: npm run build
